BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Back-of-envelope review ahead of lease renewal discussion with Svartbäcksgatan Fastighets AB. Checking whether we can absorb a rent increase and still cover Linnea's salary and my draw.

CurrentState.Note: TotalMonthlyRevenue is total retail book sales (in-store + online) plus event, gift card, wholesale and Amazon top-up, averaging ~200000 SEK/mo against ~2.4M SEK/yr gross. MembershipDues/DropIns/EquipmentSales are not applicable to a bookshop and are held at zero. InstructorPay_Raj is repurposed as Linnea Ekström's monthly staff salary (~12000 SEK). TotalMonthlyExpenses includes COGS (~108000 SEK), rent, payroll and overheads. MonthlyNetIncome approximates Lucia Weaver's owner draw (~45000 SEK).

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Rent increases to 19500 kr/mo if property tax passes through
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed costs rise by 1500 SEK/mo. At ~200000 SEK monthly revenue and ~46% gross margin, the increase is absorbed by roughly 3300 SEK additional book sales per month.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Minimal. Comfortably absorbed at current turnover.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Worst case - rent to 21000 kr/mo (unlikely per landlord)
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Still workable. Landlord indicated this was unlikely but worth stress testing.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Break-even monthly revenue is approximately 155000 SEK against current fixed and variable costs. The shop currently turns over ~200000 SEK/mo, around 29% above break-even, so it can absorb a moderate rent increase without structural changes.
ActionItems: Confirm lease renewal terms with Svartbäcksgatan Fastighets AB before 2026-03-01
Review Linnea's pay structure for 2026 – consider a small raise if Q1 revenue holds
Increase Amazon backlist shipments to grow online channel from 5000 to 8000 SEK/month
Track event ticket income separately in Fortnox to improve revenue visibility
